title: picea jezoensis subsp. microsperma
intro: Picea jezoensis subsp. microsperma, also known as the Japanese black spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its terpenoid and flavonoid compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the needles of Picea jezoensis subsp. microsperma are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
